title: Matteo Zignani | Associate Professor of Computer Science
description: Academic profile of Matteo Zignani, Associate Professor of Computer Science at the University of Milan. Research on network science, temporal graphs, graph machine learning, and socio-technical systems.

profile.name: Matteo Zignani
profile.department: Department of Computer Science “Giovanni degli Antoni”
profile.intro: I study the structure and evolution of complex networks, with a focus on methods that combine network science, data mining, and machine learning on graphs.
profile.bio: My work addresses temporal, multilayer, and heterogeneous graphs in evolving socio-technical systems. Current research spans temporal graph learning, graph neural networks, graph evolution rules, and network motifs, with applications to online communities, Web3 ecosystems, knowledge graphs, mobility data, and creative uses of generative AI.

research.title: Temporal graph learning
research.description: Representation learning and forecasting methods for dynamic networks, including link prediction and explainability.

research.title: Web3 socio-economic systems
research.description: Graph-based analysis of blockchain social platforms, stablecoin ecosystems, user migration, and anomalous behaviour.

research.title: Heterogeneous knowledge graphs
research.description: Temporal and biomedical knowledge graphs for forecasting, representation learning, and data-driven discovery.

research.title: Human mobility and social behaviour
research.description: Large-scale analysis of mobility, proximity, financial, and platform data through temporal and multilayer networks.

publications.title: Tensor factorization for temporal knowledge graph forecasting
publications.authors: Manuel Dileo, Pasquale Minervini, Matteo Zignani, and Sabrina Gaito

publications.title: User migration in blockchain-based online social networks through the lens of temporal node representation shift
Exploring time-ordered triadic closure in online social networks
publications.authors: Alessia Galdeman, Cheick Tidiane Ba, Matteo Zignani, and Sabrina Gaito

publications.title: RNA knowledge-graph analysis through homogeneous embedding methods

publications.title: Network science meets AI: A converging frontier
publications.authors: Matteo Zignani, Fragkiskos D. Malliaros, Ingo Scholtes, Roberto Interdonato, and Manuel Dileo

publications.title: Temporal graph learning for dynamic link prediction with text in online social networks

projects.name: AWESOME
projects.subtitle: Analysis framework for Web3 Social Media
projects.description: Scientific responsibility for network modelling and mining of blockchain-based social media through temporal heterogeneous graphs.

projects.name: SERICS
projects.subtitle: Security and Rights in CyberSpace
projects.description: Research on AI and machine learning methods for data protection, selective data sharing, and governance-oriented scenarios.

projects.name: CN3
projects.subtitle: National Center for Gene Therapy and Drugs based on RNA Technology
projects.description: Knowledge graphs and graph representation learning for RNA drug analysis and target prioritisation.

projects.name: GPTheatre
projects.subtitle: Generative AI for Humanities
projects.description: Design and implementation of LLM-based interaction for a multimodal theatre experiment, including iterative prompt improvement.

teaching.programme: MSc in Data Science for Economics

service: Guest Editor for Applied Network Science, Complexity, and Social Network Analysis and Mining.
Publication Chair for Complex Networks conferences from 2016 to 2026.
Program Chair for AOC 2016 and workshops at CCS 2020, CCS 2021, and ECML/PKDD 2023.
Reviewer and programme committee member for journals and conferences in network science, data mining, and machine learning.
awards: Best Runner-Up Paper Award, ACM GoodIT 2021
